what_it_does: Adds three columns to the clients table (oauth_client_id, mcp_url, bind_aud) and defines a Supabase Custom Access Token Hook function that, only for tokens minted via the registered 'Vi' OAuth client, stamps design_client claims (role, app_metadata.role/client_id/scope) and optionally a resource-bound aud; all other tokens pass through untouched.
observations: Header marks the file STAGED — no prod action — and describes its place in a flip runbook.
Adds clients.oauth_client_id, clients.mcp_url, clients.bind_aud (default false) with descriptive column comments.
Hook reads the Vi client_id, mcp_url and bind_aud from the clients row id='claude-design' status='active' (registry-is-truth, nothing hardcoded except claim VALUES).
Early return for non-oauth authentication_method and for tokens whose client_id does not match the registered Vi client (safe no-op).
Comment notes nested jsonb_set('{app_metadata,role}') does not create the parent on this PG version, so a single-level app_metadata merge is used to preserve existing metadata.
Item-B aud binding (claims.aud = mcp_url) is gated behind bind_aud=true and explicitly coordinated with the EF AUDIENCE env — comments warn never to set one without the other.
Grants EXECUTE on the hook to anon, authenticated, service_role, postgres.
standouts: The 'strictly additive, no blast radius' design: only Vi-client OAuth tokens are mutated; password/anon/other OAuth tokens are returned verbatim.
The bind_aud coupling rule — flip the column and the EF AUDIENCE env together or auth breaks — captured in column comments as an invariant.
conventions: Idempotent: add column if not exists, create or replace function.
SECURITY DEFINER + set search_path = public on the hook so it can read clients.
Verbose header + column/function comments documenting the runbook ordering and safety posture.
notes: This is the staged auth hook that turns a real Vi OAuth token into design_client claims so 0006 RLS treats it like the local service principal; it is inert until clients.oauth_client_id is populated.
questions: Has this hook actually been selected as the active Custom Access Token Hook in the dashboard (the file notes the function existing is not enough)?
Is this hook still on the claims path, given a later migration (0010) describes going native and dropping the hook?
purpose_vs_actual: Match: header says it stamps connector claims and resource-bound aud only for the Vi OAuth client's tokens, and the function does exactly that with the bind_aud gate.
apparent_themes: Registry-is-truth (claims sourced from the clients row)
Zero-blast-radius additive auth on a shared project
intention_signals: Trying to bridge a real OAuth 2.1 connector flow into the same design_client claim shape the local/RLS layer expects, while guaranteeing no other auth flow on the shared project is affected.
novelty: The single-level app_metadata merge workaround for jsonb_set not creating parents, and the explicit bind_aud↔EF-AUDIENCE coupling invariant.
contribution: Contributes the OAuth-token claim-stamping mechanism and the clients columns (oauth_client_id, mcp_url, bind_aud) that connect a verified OAuth client to the design_client RLS identity and to resource-bound audience binding.
summary_for_embedding: Supabase Custom Access Token Hook migration that stamps design_client claims and an optional resource-bound aud onto tokens from a registered Vi OAuth client, leaving all other tokens untouched.
intention_for_embedding: To let a real Claude Design OAuth login acquire the design_client claims the RLS layer gates on, additively and with optional cross-resource replay protection, as a staged flip step.
